title: Crunchy Ethiopian Potatoes Azifa
description: A traditional Ethiopian one-pot dish featuring small potatoes and herbal flavors, made in a terracotta dish with render-fat, jain-friendly and flaky, served as azifa.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large terracotta dish, heat the rendered fat over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the dish and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the dish and cook for 1 minute.
Add the halved potatoes to the dish and stir to coat with the fat and spice mixture.
Add the turmeric, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the dish and stir to combine.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven and stir in the chopped cilantro.
Return the dish to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are crispy.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
